title: cleome erosa subsp. breviglandulosa
intro: Cleome erosa subsp. breviglandulosa, also known as Mexican bee plant, is a species of flowering plant in the Cleomaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cleome erosa subsp. breviglandulosa include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
